content.problemStatement:
Chef has been given NNN arrays.

The task is to create a **strictly increasing sequence** based on the following conditions:


Chef will pick the first element randomly from any of the arrays.
From second element onwards, Chef can **equiprobably** choose an element which is:

**strictly greater** than last chosen element, and
belongs to an array other than the array of last chosen element


Find the expected sum of the resultant sequence.

Note that Chef keeps building a strictly increasing sequence as long as all conditions are met.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN  — the number of arrays.
The next NNN lines describe the arrays. The iii-th of these NNN lines contains an integer KiK_iKi​ denoting the size of the array followed by a space, then KiK_iKi​ space-separated integers denoting the array itself.




Output Format
For each test case, the expected sum can be written in the form of PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​ such that QQQ is not divisible by 109+710^9 + 7109+7. Output the value of P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 mod 109+710^9 + 7109+7 in a new line.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of KiK_iKi​ over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef either picks 444 first and then picks 101010 (since he has no other choice) or he picks 101010 first and doesn't pick any other number. So the answer is (14+10)2\frac{(14 + 10)}{2}2(14+10)​.
Test case 222: Chef can only pick 111 number (whichever he picks first), since he has no other array to pick from.
